estimands.summary: PT-identified local average level effect for units that realize dose d.
SPT-identified average level effect of assigning dose d to the treated population.
SPT-identified average causal response curve, defined as a derivative for continuous doses or an adjacent-dose slope for discrete doses.
estimands.identifying_assumption: SPT + continuous or discrete dose support

estimands.summary: Dose-distribution average of realized-dose local level effects among treated units under PT.
Dose-distribution average of ATT(d) across treated units under SPT.
estimands.source_note: Corollary line main.tex:549 prints D>d; the consistent reading is D>0 given the definition of ATT^glob and the binarized DiD discussion immediately below.

estimands.summary: Dose-density-weighted average causal response among treated units under SPT.
estimands.formula: E[ACRT(D)|D>0] with continuous or discrete plug-in aggregation

assumptions.summary: Observed two-period panel data are iid across units.
No unit is treated in period 1, period-2 doses satisfy D in {0} ∪ D+, and the untreated mass P(D=0) is positive.
Period-1 outcomes equal untreated potential outcomes and period-2 outcomes are observed at the realized dose.
Untreated potential-outcome trends are the same for each positive dose group and the untreated group.
Dose support is either continuous with positive density and differentiable mean changes or multi-valued discrete with positive probability at each support point.
For every dose d, the treated-population mean path under dose d equals the observed path of the dose-d group.

algorithm_steps.summary: Construct the first-difference outcome ΔY and split observations into untreated (D=0) and positive-dose treated units.
Estimate the untreated benchmark m0 = E_n[ΔY|D=0], which is the counterfactual anchor for level-effect estimands.
For multi-valued discrete doses, fit a saturated regression in dose indicators, interpret the coefficients as ATT(d_j), and convert adjacent contrasts into ACRT(d_j).
For continuous doses with the parametric parity path, regress treated-unit ΔY on a B-spline or polynomial basis over dvals and recover ATT(d) together with derivative-based ACRT(d).
For the CCK path, choose the data-driven sieve dimension K-hat, regress centered treated ΔY on cubic B-splines, and recover ATT_cck(d), ACRT_cck(d), and uniform bands.

Aggregate dose-specific effects with the treated-dose distribution to form ATT^glob and ACRT^glob; for staggered event-study output, average across timing groups and doses by exposure length while respecting current CCK unsupported cases.

symbol_map.description: Post-treatment dose amount, constant within unit across periods and zeroed out for never-treated units.
First-difference outcome Y_post - Y_pre used as the basic regression target.
PT-identified local level-effect curve evaluated at the realized dose group.
SPT-identified global level-effect curve among treated units.
SPT-identified marginal response curve, implemented as a derivative or adjacent-dose slope.
Cubic B-spline basis evaluated at dose d for ATT(d) estimation.
Derivative of the spline basis used to map the fitted curve into ACRT(d).
Data-driven CCK sieve dimension chosen before constructing the spline basis and confidence bands.

notes: Treat point estimates, standard errors, confidence bands, summary aggregates, and sign/monotonic diagnostics as separate tolerance families.
Do not relax tolerances without updating automation/contracts/numerical-truth-contract.md and attaching fresh rerun evidence.
Use tolerance_families as the machine-readable gate registry before any parity or Monte Carlo test sets fixture-level tolerances.

tolerance_families.calibration_rule: Set fixture-level atol/rtol from contdid-r parity outputs before asserting dose-level estimator equality.
Set fixture-level atol/rtol from bootstrap or analytic-inference parity outputs before asserting standard-error equality.
Set fixture-level atol/rtol from inference-band parity outputs before asserting confidence-interval equality.
Set fixture-level atol/rtol from treated-dose aggregation parity outputs before asserting summary-effect equality.
Support membership, sign restrictions, and monotonic-segment diagnostics must match exactly; do not relax this gate via numeric tolerances.
Event-time labels, ordering, and exposure-length semantics must match exactly; parity runs should fail on any index drift.
